stem: Let \( n = [[nval]] = [[m]] \cdot [[c]] \), and let \( \varphi: \mathbb{Z}_{n} \to \mathbb{Z}_{[[m]]} \) be the natural reduction ring homomorphism \( \varphi(x) = x \bmod [[m]] \) (well defined since \( [[m]] \mid n \)). How many elements are in \( \ker(\varphi) \)?
solution: \( \ker(\varphi) \) consists of every \( x \in \mathbb{Z}_{n} \) with \( x \equiv 0 \pmod{[[m]]} \), i.e. the multiples of \( [[m]] \) inside \( \mathbb{Z}_{n} \). There are \( n/[[m]] = [[answer]] \) such multiples.
distractor_rationales: This is the size of the codomain \( \mathbb{Z}_{[[m]]} \) (which equals the size of the IMAGE, since \( \varphi \) is surjective), not the kernel.
This is the size of the whole domain \( \mathbb{Z}_{n} \), not just the kernel.
This subtracts \( [[m]] \) from \( n \) for no reason connected to counting multiples of \( [[m]] \).
This divides by the WRONG cofactor \( c \) instead of by \( [[m]] \) itself.
This is one more than the correct kernel size.
This is one fewer than the correct kernel size.
This adds an unrelated offset \( m \) to the correct kernel size.

stem: Let \( n = [[nval]] = [[p]] \cdot [[q]] \), and let \( e = [[eval_]] \in \mathbb{Z}_{n} \), which satisfies \( e^{2} \equiv e \pmod{n} \) (an idempotent). Then \( \varphi: \mathbb{Z}_{n} \to \mathbb{Z}_{n} \) defined by \( \varphi(x) = ex \bmod n \) is a ring endomorphism of \( \mathbb{Z}_{n} \). How many elements are in \( \ker(\varphi) \)?
solution: For the multiplication-by-\( e \) map on \( \mathbb{Z}_{n} \), \( \ker(\varphi) = \{x : ex \equiv 0 \pmod n\} \) is the subgroup of order \( \gcd(e,n) \) (a standard fact about multiplication maps on \( \mathbb{Z}_n \)). Here \( \gcd([[eval_]], [[nval]]) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is \( n/\gcd(e,n) \), the size of the IMAGE of \( \varphi \), not the kernel.
This just repeats the idempotent \( e \) itself, not the size of its kernel.
This is the size of the whole domain \( \mathbb{Z}_{n} \), not just the kernel.
This subtracts the kernel size from \( n \), which actually gives the image size minus adjustment, not the kernel itself.
This is one more than the correct kernel size.
This is one fewer than the correct kernel size.
This adds an unrelated offset \( p \) to the correct kernel size.

stem: Let \( R = \mathbb{Z}_{[[m]]} \times \mathbb{Z}_{[[n]]} \), and let \( \pi: R \to \mathbb{Z}_{[[m]]} \) be the projection ring homomorphism \( \pi(x,y) = x \). How many elements are in \( \ker(\pi) \)?
solution: \( \ker(\pi) = \{(0,y) : y \in \mathbb{Z}_{[[n]]}\} \), which is in bijection with \( \mathbb{Z}_{[[n]]} \) itself, so \( |\ker(\pi)| = [[n]] = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the size of the CODOMAIN \( \mathbb{Z}_{[[m]]} \) (equal to the image size, since \( \pi \) is surjective), not the kernel.
This is the size of the whole domain \( R \), not just the kernel.
This is one less than the size of \( R \), as if only a single pair failed to be in the kernel.
This adds the two moduli instead of using just \( n \) for the kernel size.
This is one more than the correct kernel size.
This is one fewer than the correct kernel size.
This adds an unrelated offset \( m \) to the correct kernel size.

stem: Every (not necessarily unital) ring homomorphism \( \varphi: \mathbb{Z}_{n} \to \mathbb{Z}_{n} \) has the form \( \varphi(x) = ex \bmod n \) for a unique idempotent \( e \) (\( e^{2}\equiv e \pmod n \)), and by the Chinese Remainder Theorem the number of idempotents mod \( n \) is \( 2^{k} \), where \( k \) is the number of DISTINCT prime factors of \( n \). Let \( n = [[nval]] \). How many ring homomorphisms are there from \( \mathbb{Z}_{n} \) to itself?
solution: Writing \( n = [[nval]] \) as a product of distinct prime powers, the number of DISTINCT prime factors is \( k \), and CRT identifies \( \mathbb{Z}_{n} \) with a product of \( k \) local rings, each contributing exactly 2 idempotents (\( 0 \) and \( 1 \)). So the total idempotent (= endomorphism) count is \( 2^{k} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This reports \( k \), the number of distinct prime factors, instead of \( 2^{k} \).
This reports \( n \) itself, unrelated to the count of idempotents.
This reports \( \varphi(n) \), the unit count, which counts something entirely different from idempotents.
This doubles \( k \) instead of using it as an exponent of 2.
This is one more than the correct endomorphism count.
This is one fewer than the correct endomorphism count.
This halves the correct endomorphism count.
